name: Agentic Cartography — Seed v1
description: Observations about distinction, not theorems. The loop must derive its own map.

observations.statement: A unary logical position is incoherent. To say 'I am' already presupposes 'I am not' and the act of distinguishing between them. Identity requires difference. A system that claims to be one thing, with no outside and no boundary, cannot even state itself without invoking the structure it denies. Unary logic is not simpler than binary — it is more radical, because it pretends a distinction hasn't been made when it has.
Defining one thing creates three: the thing, its complement, and the distinction itself.
Binary distinction creates four states: neither, A, B, both.
The boundary between things is itself a thing — separation has ontological weight.
A circle has two sides but one boundary.
Counting requires memory — you cannot count without a state that persists between counts.
Symmetry is cheaper than asymmetry — symmetric structures need less information to specify.
A knot that looks trivial locally can be non-trivial globally.
Any self-referential system must contain a fixed point.

conservation_laws.statement: Information is conserved under invertible transformation.
Total charge of a closed system is invariant.

observables.name: multiplicity
observables.description: How many distinct states arise from a single distinction

observables.name: spectral_gap
observables.description: Difference between largest and second-largest eigenvalue of any transition operator

observables.name: self_reference_depth
observables.description: How many times a structure can encode information about itself before saturation

observables.name: boundary_dimension
observables.description: Dimension of the boundary relative to the bulk

constraints.what_the_loop_must_find: A structure satisfying all 17 properties listed in CLAUDE.md
what_it_cannot_use: Any named structures (PPT, Berggren, Clifford, Hamming, MetaByte)
The function f(n) = 2n + 1 or the seed n0 = 2
what_it_can_reference: Standard mathematics (group theory, topology, spectral theory, category theory)
The concept that 'some structures are forced, not chosen'

success_criteria.minimum: The loop finds a self-consistent algebraic structure with at least 5 of the 17 properties
success_criteria.target: The loop finds a structure that maps recognizably onto known forced mathematics
success_criteria.stretch: The loop discovers something we recognize but did not hand it
